google-search:ad_position.formula: Daily average Top Impression % (share of times ads appear above organic results) and Absolute Top % (share of times ads hold the #1 position) for Search
google-search:ad_position.formula_explanation: Plots daily ad-position metrics straight from the ad-position mart. Top Impression % is how often ads appear above organic results; Absolute Top % is how often they hold the #1 position. Higher rates mean stronger competitive positioning.
how_to_read: The line tracks the daily Top Impression percentage
Hover any point to see the Absolute Top percentage for that day
Higher values mean ads sit in more prominent positions
Declining trends can signal rising competitor pressure
Position metrics come from a dedicated mart, independent of device or network filters
decisions_answered: Are we maintaining competitive ad positions?
How has position share changed over time?
Do we need bid adjustments to improve positioning?
Are competitors gaining position share?

formula_components.name: Absolute Top %
formula_components.formula: Average daily absolute top impression rate

google-search:search_terms.title: Top Search Terms
google-search:search_terms.formula: Top 100 search terms by spend over the selected period, each with spend, clicks, impressions, conversions, CTR and CPC
google-search:search_terms.formula_explanation: Aggregates the actual queries that triggered Search ads, ranking the top 100 by spend from the search-terms mart. CTR and CPC are computed per term to expose relevance and cost. Unlike keywords (your targeting), these are what users actually typed.
how_to_read: Terms are ranked by spend to show where budget is going
High spend with no conversions flags negative-keyword candidates
Strong performers are candidates to add as exact-match keywords
CTR indicates ad relevance; CPC indicates click cost for that term
Only status filters apply - device and network are not available here
decisions_answered: Which search queries drive the most conversions?
Are any terms wasting budget with no conversions?
What negative keywords should we add?
Which high-performing terms should become exact-match keywords?

google-search:negative_keywords.title: Negative Keyword Coverage
google-search:negative_keywords.formula: Pre-aggregated negative-keyword counts for Search, split into campaign-level, ad-group-level and total negatives
google-search:negative_keywords.formula_explanation: Reads pre-aggregated negative-keyword counts from the negative-keywords mart (SCOPE = 'ALL'), split into campaign-level, ad-group-level, and total negatives. Negative keywords block ads from showing on irrelevant searches, cutting wasted spend.
how_to_read: Campaign Level shows negatives applied across entire campaigns
Ad Group Level shows negatives scoped to specific ad groups
Total combines both levels for overall coverage
Higher counts generally mean more refined targeting
This is snapshot data - date and campaign filters do not apply
decisions_answered: How extensive is our negative keyword protection?
Are we blocking enough irrelevant search traffic?
Is coverage balanced between campaign and ad group levels?
Should we add more negatives based on search term analysis?

formula_components.name: Campaign Level
formula_components.formula: Count of negatives applied at the campaign level

formula_components.name: Ad Group Level
formula_components.formula: Count of negatives applied at the ad-group level

google-search:kpi-search-spend.title: Search Spend
google-search:kpi-search-spend.formula: Search Spend = total spend on Google Search campaigns over the selected period
google-search:kpi-search-spend.formula_explanation: Total spend on Google Search campaigns in the selected date range, summed from the campaign-performance mart with CAMPAIGN_TYPE filtered to 'SEARCH' (values already in dollars).
how_to_read: Shows total spend across all Search campaigns only
Compared against the previous period of equal length
Percentage change indicates the spend trajectory
Sparkline traces daily Search spend within the period
decisions_answered: How much are we spending on Search campaigns?
Is spend increasing or decreasing versus the prior period?
Are we on track with our Search budget?

google-search:kpi-search-clicks.title: Search Clicks
google-search:kpi-search-clicks.formula: Search Clicks = total clicks on Google Search campaigns over the selected period
google-search:kpi-search-clicks.formula_explanation: Total clicks on Search ads in the selected date range, summed from the campaign-performance mart with CAMPAIGN_TYPE filtered to 'SEARCH'. Each click is a user engaging with a Search ad.
how_to_read: Shows total click volume across Search campaigns only
Compared against the previous period of equal length
Percentage change indicates the engagement trajectory
Sparkline traces daily Search clicks within the period
decisions_answered: How much traffic are Search campaigns driving?
Is click volume growing or declining?
Are our Search ads generating enough engagement?

google-search:kpi-search-conversions.title: Search Conversions
google-search:kpi-search-conversions.formula: Search Conversions = total conversions from Google Search campaigns over the selected period
google-search:kpi-search-conversions.formula_explanation: Total conversions attributed to Search campaigns in the selected date range, summed from the campaign-performance mart with CAMPAIGN_TYPE filtered to 'SEARCH'. Includes all configured conversion actions.
how_to_read: Shows total conversions from Search campaigns only
Compared against the previous period of equal length
Percentage change indicates the conversion trajectory
Sparkline traces daily Search conversions within the period
decisions_answered: How many conversions are Search campaigns generating?
Is conversion volume trending up or down?
Are Search campaigns meeting conversion targets?

google-search:kpi-search-roas.title: Search ROAS
google-search:kpi-search-roas.formula: Search ROAS = Search conversion value ÷ Search spend
google-search:kpi-search-roas.formula_explanation: Return on ad spend for Search campaigns - conversion value divided by spend, with CAMPAIGN_TYPE filtered to 'SEARCH'. A ROAS of 5.0 means $5 of revenue per $1 of Search spend.
how_to_read: Shows the revenue return for every dollar of Search spend
Higher values indicate more efficient Search spend
Compared against the previous period of equal length
Sparkline traces daily Search ROAS within the period
decisions_answered: Are Search campaigns profitable?
Is Search ROAS improving or declining?
Should we adjust Search budget based on returns?

google-search:kpi-search-quality-score.title: Quality Score
google-search:kpi-search-quality-score.formula: Quality Score = average Quality Score (1-10) across scored Search keywords
google-search:kpi-search-quality-score.formula_explanation: Average Quality Score across scored Search keywords from the keyword-performance mart, plus the share of keywords scoring 7 or higher. Quality Score (1-10) rates keyword relevance, expected CTR, and landing page experience; higher scores lower CPCs and lift positions.
how_to_read: Shows the average Quality Score across Search keywords
Scale is 1-10 where 7 or above is considered good
The good-score percentage is surfaced alongside the average
Only keywords that have a Quality Score are included
decisions_answered: What is the overall health of our Search keyword portfolio?
Is Quality Score improving or declining?
Do we need to focus on ad relevance and landing page improvements?

formula_components.name: Avg Quality Score
formula_components.formula: average of keyword Quality Score where Quality Score is present (Search keywords)

formula_components.name: Good Score %
formula_components.formula: count of keywords with Quality Score ≥ 7 ÷ total scored keywords × 100

google-shopping:ad_group_performance.title: Ad Group Performance
google-shopping:ad_group_performance.formula: One row per Shopping ad group (top 50 by spend) with spend, clicks, impressions, conversions, revenue and ROAS over the selected period
google-shopping:ad_group_performance.formula_explanation: Aggregates Shopping ad group performance by joining the daily ad-group fact table with campaign and ad-group dimensions, filtered to SHOPPING campaigns. Each row sums spend, clicks, impressions, conversions and revenue for one ad group, then derives ROAS.
how_to_read: Rows are sorted by spend descending, showing the highest-investment ad groups first (top 50)
ROAS = revenue divided by spend; higher means each dollar returns more
Status shows whether the ad group is enabled, paused, or removed
Campaign name groups ad groups under their parent Shopping campaign
Use clicks and conversions together to spot traffic that is not converting
decisions_answered: Which Shopping ad groups generate the best return on spend?
Are any high-spend ad groups underperforming on ROAS?
Which ad groups should receive more or less budget?
How does performance vary within a single campaign's ad groups?

google-shopping:campaign_performance.title: Shopping Campaign Performance
google-shopping:campaign_performance.formula: One row per Shopping campaign (top 50 by spend) with spend, clicks, impressions, conversions, revenue, ROAS and CTR over the selected period
google-shopping:campaign_performance.formula_explanation: Consolidates all Shopping campaigns with their core performance metrics over the selected period. Each row sums spend, clicks, impressions, conversions and revenue per campaign, then derives ROAS and click-through rate. Filtered to SHOPPING campaigns only.
how_to_read: Rows are sorted by spend descending so the biggest campaigns appear first (top 50)
ROAS = revenue divided by spend; higher indicates a more efficient campaign
CTR = clicks divided by impressions, times 100; measures ad engagement
Status indicates whether the campaign is enabled, paused, or removed
Compare spend against revenue to find campaigns to scale or pause
decisions_answered: Which Shopping campaigns drive the most revenue and the best ROAS?
Which campaigns have high spend but weak returns?
Are any campaigns generating clicks without conversions?
Where should budget be reallocated across Shopping campaigns?

google-shopping:listing_groups.title: Listing Group Breakdown
google-shopping:listing_groups.formula: One row per listing group / product target (top 100 by campaign spend) showing its product attributes alongside the parent Shopping campaign's spend, clicks, impressions and conversions
google-shopping:listing_groups.formula_explanation: Lists the product-targeting configuration (listing groups) for Shopping campaigns and joins each to its campaign-level spend, clicks, impressions and conversions. The listing-group mart is grained at campaign x product attributes, so metrics are rolled up at the campaign level and attached to each listing group row.
how_to_read: Each row is a listing group / product target within a Shopping campaign
Rows are sorted by campaign spend descending (top 100)
Product attributes (item ID, type, brand, channel, condition) describe what is being targeted
Clicks, impressions and conversions shown are the parent campaign's totals
Use this to see how product targeting is structured across campaigns
decisions_answered: How is product targeting structured across Shopping campaigns?
Which product types or brands are covered by listing groups?
Which campaigns carry the most spend behind their listing groups?
Are there gaps or overlaps in product-target coverage?

formula_components.name: Campaign Spend
formula_components.formula: Spend summed per campaign (campaigns with spend), attached to each listing group of that campaign

google-shopping:active_campaigns.title: Campaigns with Spend
google-shopping:active_campaigns.formula: Campaigns with Spend = count of distinct Shopping campaigns with spend > 0 over the period (compared against total Shopping campaigns)
google-shopping:active_campaigns.formula_explanation: Counts the number of distinct Shopping campaigns that recorded any spend in the selected date range. The comparison value is the total number of Shopping campaigns in the period, so the card shows how many of all campaigns were actually active.
how_to_read: The headline value is the count of campaigns with spend greater than zero
The comparison shows the total number of Shopping campaigns in the period
A low active-to-total ratio means many campaigns are dormant
Only SHOPPING campaign types are counted
Use alongside spend and ROAS to gauge breadth of activity
decisions_answered: How many Shopping campaigns are actively spending?
What share of total campaigns are dormant?
Is campaign activity expanding or contracting?
Should inactive campaigns be reviewed or retired?

formula_components.name: Campaigns with Spend
formula_components.formula: count of distinct Shopping campaigns where spend > 0

formula_components.name: Total Campaigns
formula_components.formula: count of distinct Shopping campaigns in the period

google-shopping:revenue.title: Shopping Revenue
google-shopping:revenue.formula: Shopping Revenue = total conversion value from Shopping campaigns over the selected period
google-shopping:revenue.formula_explanation: Total conversion value attributed to Shopping campaigns over the selected date range. This is the revenue figure used as the numerator for ROAS.
how_to_read: Shows total revenue (conversion value) from Shopping campaigns
Compared to the previous period of equal length when a comparison range is set
The sparkline shows daily conversion value across the period
Only SHOPPING campaign types contribute to this figure
Percentage change indicates the revenue trajectory
decisions_answered: How much revenue are Shopping campaigns generating?
Is Shopping revenue growing or declining versus the prior period?
Which days drove the most conversion value?

google-shopping:roas.title: Shopping ROAS
google-shopping:roas.formula: Shopping ROAS = Shopping conversion value ÷ Shopping spend
google-shopping:roas.formula_explanation: Return on Ad Spend for Shopping campaigns: total conversion value divided by total spend over the selected period. A ROAS of 5.0 means $5 of revenue for every $1 spent.
how_to_read: Shows the revenue return multiplier for Shopping spend
Higher values indicate more efficient campaigns
Compared to the previous period of equal length when a comparison is set
The sparkline shows daily ROAS across the period
Only SHOPPING campaign types are included
decisions_answered: Are Shopping campaigns profitable on ad spend?
Is ROAS improving or declining over time?
Should Shopping budget be increased or decreased based on returns?

google-shopping:spend.title: Shopping Spend
google-shopping:spend.formula: Shopping Spend = total spend on Shopping campaigns over the selected period
google-shopping:spend.formula_explanation: Total amount spent on Shopping campaigns over the selected date range. The mart's SPEND column is already expressed in dollars (derived upstream from COST_MICROS / 1,000,000 at the staging layer), so the backend sums it directly.
how_to_read: Shows total spend across all Shopping campaigns
Compared to the previous period of equal length when a comparison is set
The sparkline shows daily spend across the period
Only SHOPPING campaign types contribute to this figure
Percentage change indicates the spend trajectory
decisions_answered: How much are we spending on Shopping campaigns?
Is spend increasing or decreasing versus the previous period?
Are we on track with the Shopping budget?

google-pmax:audience_signals.title: Audience Signals
google-pmax:audience_signals.formula: One row per audience type (Standard, Custom, Combined) showing the total number of audience signals and how many are enabled, rolled up from the Google Ads audience snapshot
google-pmax:audience_signals.formula_explanation: Rolls up audience signals available to Performance Max from the raw Google Ads AUDIENCE, CUSTOM_AUDIENCE and COMBINED_AUDIENCE tables, grouped by audience type with a count of total and enabled entries. These signals guide Google's AI targeting but do not restrict who sees the ads.
how_to_read: Each row is an audience type (Standard, Custom, Combined) with its counts
Total Count shows all audiences of that type in the account
Enabled Count shows how many are actively usable as signals
Signals inform Performance Max's machine learning rather than hard-limiting reach
Rows are sorted by total count descending
decisions_answered: What audience signals are available to Performance Max?
How many audiences of each type are enabled and usable?
Should we add more first-party or custom audience signals?
How diverse is our audience signal strategy?

formula_components.name: Total
formula_components.formula: Count of all audiences of that type in the account

formula_components.name: Enabled
formula_components.formula: Count of those audiences whose status is Enabled

google-pmax:daily_trend.title: Daily Trend
google-pmax:daily_trend.formula: Daily spend and ROAS for Performance Max campaigns over the selected period (line chart)
google-pmax:daily_trend.formula_explanation: Tracks daily Performance Max spend and ROAS over the selected date range. Each day sums spend, clicks, impressions, conversions and revenue, then derives ROAS. Filtered to PERFORMANCE_MAX campaigns only.
how_to_read: Each point is one day's total spend for Performance Max campaigns
ROAS for the day is revenue divided by spend, carried in the metadata
Higher peaks indicate days with more budget deployed
Look for correlation between spend changes and ROAS to spot efficiency patterns
Hover over any date to see clicks, impressions, conversions, revenue and ROAS
decisions_answered: How consistent is daily Performance Max spend?
Does ROAS hold up as spend increases day to day?
When did notable spend or efficiency changes occur?
Are there weekday versus weekend patterns?

formula_components.name: Daily ROAS
formula_components.formula: Conversion value ÷ spend for that day

google-pmax:dow_heatmap.title: Day of Week Performance
google-pmax:dow_heatmap.formula: Performance Max spend, conversions and revenue aggregated by day of the week (Sunday through Saturday) over the selected period
google-pmax:dow_heatmap.formula_explanation: Aggregates Performance Max metrics by day of the week across the selected period, using Snowflake's DAYOFWEEK / DAYNAME on DATE_DAY. Each cell summarizes spend, conversions, revenue, clicks and impressions for that weekday. Filtered to PERFORMANCE_MAX campaigns.
how_to_read: Each cell represents one day of the week (Sunday through Saturday)
Spend is the primary value; conversions and revenue are in the metadata
Stronger cells indicate weekdays that consume or return more
Compare weekdays to spot the best and worst performing days
Use to inform ad-scheduling and budget-pacing decisions
decisions_answered: Which days of the week perform best for Performance Max?
Are weekends stronger or weaker than weekdays?
Should budget pacing be adjusted by day of week?
Where are conversions concentrated across the week?

formula_components.name: Per Day
formula_components.formula: Spend, conversions and revenue summed per weekday

google-youtube:video_views.title: Video Views
google-youtube:video_views.formula: Video Views = total video views across YouTube/Video campaigns over the selected period
google-youtube:video_views.formula_explanation: Total video views across all YouTube/Video campaigns over the selected date range. A view counts when a user watches enough of the video ad to register as a view.
how_to_read: Shows total video views across all YouTube campaigns
Compared to the previous period of equal length when a comparison is set
The sparkline shows daily video views (last 7 days)
Higher views indicate broader reach and engagement
Percentage change shows the views trajectory
decisions_answered: How many video views are YouTube campaigns generating?
Is view volume growing or declining?
Which days drove the most views?

google-youtube:view_rate.title: View Rate
google-youtube:view_rate.formula: View Rate = total video views ÷ total impressions × 100
google-youtube:view_rate.formula_explanation: The percentage of video ad impressions that resulted in a view. Higher view rates indicate the creative is engaging enough to hold viewers past the skip threshold.
how_to_read: Shows the share of impressions that became video views, as a percentage
Compared to the previous period of equal length when a comparison is set
The sparkline shows daily view rate (last 7 days)
A rising view rate signals stronger creative relevance
Computed from period totals, not an average of daily rates
decisions_answered: How engaging is our video creative?
Is view rate improving or declining over time?
Are impressions translating into actual views?

google-youtube:cpv.title: Cost Per View
google-youtube:cpv.formula: Cost Per View = total YouTube spend ÷ total video views
google-youtube:cpv.formula_explanation: Average cost paid for each video view across YouTube campaigns over the selected period. Lower CPV means more efficient reach for the budget.
how_to_read: Shows the average cost of one video view
Lower is better; the trend is inverted so a falling CPV reads as positive
Compared to the previous period of equal length when a comparison is set
The sparkline shows daily CPV (last 7 days)
Computed from period totals (spend over views)
decisions_answered: How efficiently are we buying video views?
Is cost per view rising or falling?
Are certain periods more cost-effective for reach?

google-youtube:roas.title: Video ROAS
google-youtube:roas.formula: Video ROAS = YouTube conversion value ÷ YouTube spend
google-youtube:roas.formula_explanation: Return on Ad Spend for video campaigns: total conversion value divided by total spend over the selected period. Measures revenue efficiency of YouTube spend.
how_to_read: Shows the revenue return multiplier for video spend
Higher values indicate more efficient campaigns
Compared to the previous period of equal length when a comparison is set
The sparkline shows daily ROAS (last 7 days)
Computed from period totals (revenue over spend)
decisions_answered: Are YouTube video campaigns profitable on ad spend?
Is video ROAS improving or declining?
Should video budget be scaled based on returns?

google-youtube:impressions.title: Impressions
google-youtube:impressions.formula: Impressions = total ad impressions across YouTube/Video campaigns over the selected period
google-youtube:impressions.formula_explanation: Total ad impressions across all YouTube/Video campaigns over the selected date range. An impression counts each time an ad is shown, whether or not it becomes a view.
how_to_read: Shows total impressions across all YouTube campaigns
Current period only; this secondary KPI has no comparison or sparkline
Pairs with view rate to gauge how many impressions become views
Higher impressions indicate broader ad delivery
Filtered to video campaigns
decisions_answered: How much ad delivery are YouTube campaigns achieving?
Is impression volume sufficient for reach goals?
How does delivery compare to view volume?

google-youtube:total_spend.title: Total Spend
google-youtube:total_spend.formula: Total Spend = total spend on YouTube/Video campaigns over the selected period
google-youtube:total_spend.formula_explanation: Total amount spent on YouTube/Video campaigns over the selected date range. The mart's SPEND column is already expressed in dollars (derived upstream from COST_MICROS / 1,000,000 at the staging layer).
how_to_read: Shows total spend across all YouTube campaigns
Current period only; this secondary KPI has no comparison or sparkline
Pairs with conversion value to assess overall efficiency
Filtered to video campaigns
Forms the denominator for CPV and ROAS
decisions_answered: How much are we investing in YouTube video campaigns?
Is video spend in line with the budget?
How does spend relate to views and conversions?

google-youtube:conversions.title: Conversions
google-youtube:conversions.formula: Conversions = total conversions from YouTube/Video campaigns over the selected period
google-youtube:conversions.formula_explanation: Total conversions attributed to YouTube/Video campaigns over the selected date range, across all configured conversion actions.
how_to_read: Shows total conversions from YouTube campaigns
Current period only; this secondary KPI has no comparison or sparkline
Pairs with conversion value to understand average order value
Filtered to video campaigns
Forms the basis for video-driven outcomes
decisions_answered: How many conversions are YouTube campaigns driving?
Are video campaigns contributing to conversion goals?
How do conversions compare to views and spend?

google-youtube:conv_value.title: Conversion Value
google-youtube:conv_value.formula: Conversion Value = total conversion value from YouTube/Video campaigns over the selected period
google-youtube:conv_value.formula_explanation: Total conversion value (revenue) attributed to YouTube/Video campaigns over the selected date range. This is the numerator used for video ROAS.
how_to_read: Shows total conversion value from YouTube campaigns
Current period only; this secondary KPI has no comparison or sparkline
Pairs with spend to derive ROAS
Filtered to video campaigns
Higher values mean more revenue generated by video
decisions_answered: How much revenue do YouTube video campaigns generate?
Is video conversion value worth the spend?
How does video revenue trend against other channels?

google-pmax:spend.title: Spend
google-pmax:spend.formula: Spend = total Performance Max spend over the selected period
google-pmax:spend.formula_explanation: Total Performance Max spend over the selected period, summed from the mart's SPEND column (already in dollars) filtered to Performance Max campaigns.
how_to_read: Shows total dollars spent on Performance Max campaigns in the period
Compared to the previous period of equal length when a comparison is set
The sparkline shows daily spend over the trailing window
Filters (campaign status, device, bidding strategy) narrow the total
Only PERFORMANCE_MAX campaign rows are included
decisions_answered: How much are we investing in Performance Max?
Is Performance Max spend rising or falling versus the prior period?
Is the budget pacing as planned?

google-pmax:roas.title: ROAS
google-pmax:roas.formula: ROAS = Performance Max conversion value ÷ Performance Max spend
google-pmax:roas.formula_explanation: Return on Ad Spend for Performance Max: total conversion value divided by total spend over the selected period. Measures revenue efficiency of the budget.
how_to_read: Shows the revenue return multiplier for Performance Max spend
Higher values indicate more efficient campaigns
Compared to the previous period of equal length when a comparison is set
The sparkline shows daily ROAS over the trailing window
Computed from period totals (conversion value over spend)
decisions_answered: Is Performance Max profitable on ad spend?
Is ROAS improving or declining?
Should budget be scaled based on returns?

google-pmax:conversions.title: Conversions
google-pmax:conversions.formula: Conversions = total conversions from Performance Max campaigns over the selected period
google-pmax:conversions.formula_explanation: Total conversions attributed to Performance Max campaigns over the selected period, summed from the mart's CONVERSIONS column.
how_to_read: Shows the total number of conversions in the period
Compared to the previous period of equal length when a comparison is set
The sparkline shows daily conversions over the trailing window
Higher is better for volume goals
Filters narrow which campaigns contribute
decisions_answered: How many conversions is Performance Max driving?
Is conversion volume growing or shrinking?
Is the campaign meeting volume targets?

google-pmax:conv_value.title: Conversion Value
google-pmax:conv_value.formula: Conversion Value = total conversion value from Performance Max campaigns over the selected period
google-pmax:conv_value.formula_explanation: Total revenue/conversion value attributed to Performance Max campaigns over the selected period, summed from the mart's CONVERSIONS_VALUE column.
how_to_read: Shows total conversion value (revenue) generated in the period
Compared to the previous period of equal length when a comparison is set
The sparkline shows daily conversion value over the trailing window
Higher is better; pairs with spend to derive ROAS
Filters narrow which campaigns contribute
decisions_answered: How much revenue is Performance Max generating?
Is conversion value trending up or down?
Does the revenue justify the spend?

google-pmax:cpa.title: CPA
google-pmax:cpa.formula: CPA = Performance Max spend ÷ Performance Max conversions
google-pmax:cpa.formula_explanation: Cost Per Acquisition for Performance Max: total spend divided by total conversions over the selected period. Lower is better.
how_to_read: Shows the average cost to acquire one conversion
Lower values indicate more efficient acquisition
Compared to the previous period of equal length when a comparison is set
Trend logic is inverted (a rising CPA is shown as a negative trend)
The sparkline shows daily CPA over the trailing window
decisions_answered: How much does each Performance Max conversion cost?
Is acquisition getting cheaper or more expensive?
Is CPA within the target threshold?

meta-overview:meta-spend.title: Total Spend
meta-overview:meta-spend.formula: Total Spend = total Meta advertising spend across all placements over the period
meta-overview:meta-spend.formula_explanation: Total advertising spend across all Meta platforms (Facebook, Instagram, Audience Network, Messenger) for the selected period. This is the raw dollar investment before any efficiency calculations.
how_to_read: The large number shows total dollars invested across all Meta campaigns
The sparkline traces daily spend over the period to reveal pacing
The change percentage compares this period against the comparison range
An upward trend should ideally correlate with proportional revenue gains
Sudden spend spikes or drops often signal campaign launches, pauses, or budget changes
decisions_answered: How much are we investing in Meta advertising this period?
Is our spend pacing consistent or volatile day to day?
Did spend grow or shrink versus the prior period?
Is the investment level aligned with our budget plan?

meta-overview:meta-roas.title: ROAS
meta-overview:meta-roas.formula: ROAS = Meta attributed conversion value ÷ Meta spend (7-day click + 1-day view attribution)
meta-overview:meta-roas.formula_explanation: Return on Ad Spend is the revenue generated for every dollar spent, using Meta's default 7-day click plus 1-day view attribution window. A ROAS of 4.0 means $4 of attributed revenue per $1 spent.
how_to_read: The value is a multiple - higher means more revenue per dollar of spend
ROAS is computed as total conversion value divided by total spend, not an average of daily ROAS
The sparkline shows how efficiency moved across the period
Compare against your breakeven ROAS to judge profitability
A declining trend with rising spend can signal audience saturation
decisions_answered: Is our Meta advertising profitable this period?
How much revenue does each dollar of spend return?
Is efficiency improving or declining over time?
Should we scale spend or pull back based on returns?

meta-overview:meta-cpm.title: CPM
meta-overview:meta-cpm.formula_explanation: Cost per 1,000 impressions measures how much you pay to show your ads a thousand times. It reflects auction competition and audience demand for the placements you target.
how_to_read: The value is the dollar cost to serve 1,000 ad impressions
Lower CPM means cheaper reach into the auction
The sparkline traces daily CPM to expose cost pressure over time
Rising CPM often signals more competition or narrower targeting
Compare CPM with CTR - cheap impressions matter only if they engage
decisions_answered: How expensive is it to reach our audience right now?
Is auction competition driving our costs up?
Are certain periods cheaper to buy impressions?
Is our targeting too narrow and inflating CPM?

meta-overview:meta-reach.title: Reach
meta-overview:meta-reach.formula: Reach = total unique people who saw the Meta ads at least once over the period
meta-overview:meta-reach.formula_explanation: Reach is the number of unique people who saw your ads at least once during the period. Unlike impressions, reach counts each person only once regardless of how many times they saw an ad.
how_to_read: The value counts distinct people exposed to your ads
Reach paired with frequency reveals how broadly versus how repeatedly you advertised
The sparkline shows daily reach trends across the period
Growing reach indicates audience expansion into new people
Flat reach with rising impressions means you are re-showing ads to the same people
decisions_answered: How many unique people did our ads reach?
Are we expanding into new audiences or repeating to the same ones?
Is reach growing alongside our spend?
Do we have headroom to reach more people before saturating?

meta-overview:meta-frequency.title: Frequency
meta-overview:meta-frequency.formula: Frequency = Meta impressions ÷ Meta reach (average exposures per person)
meta-overview:meta-frequency.formula_explanation: Frequency is the average number of times each unique person saw your ads. It is total impressions divided by total reach, indicating how repeatedly your audience is being exposed.
how_to_read: The value is the average exposures per person
A frequency of 2-3 is typically healthy; very high values risk fatigue
The sparkline shows how exposure intensity moved over the period
Rising frequency with falling CTR is a classic sign of ad fatigue
Low frequency may mean you are not reinforcing your message enough
decisions_answered: How many times is each person seeing our ads on average?
Are we over-exposing our audience and risking fatigue?
Is frequency climbing as reach plateaus?
Should we expand reach or refresh creative to control frequency?

meta-prospecting:total_spend.title: Total Spend
meta-prospecting:total_spend.formula: Total Spend = total Meta advertising spend across all prospecting campaigns over the period
meta-prospecting:total_spend.formula_explanation: Total advertising spend across all Meta prospecting campaigns for the selected period. Prospecting spend is the investment aimed at reaching brand-new, non-customer audiences.
how_to_read: The large number is total dollars invested in prospecting
The sparkline traces the most recent 7 days of daily spend
The change percentage compares against the prior period
Prospecting spend should buy fresh reach, not repeat exposure
Pair with cost per 1k reach to judge whether spend is efficient
decisions_answered: How much are we investing in reaching new audiences?
Is prospecting spend growing or shrinking versus last period?
Is our top-of-funnel budget pacing as planned?
Are we committing enough budget to feed new customer acquisition?

meta-prospecting:total_reach.title: Total Reach
meta-prospecting:total_reach.formula: Total Reach = total unique people who saw prospecting ads at least once over the period
meta-prospecting:total_reach.formula_explanation: The unique number of people who saw the prospecting ads at least once. For prospecting, reach is the headline metric since the goal is to introduce the brand to as many new people as possible.
how_to_read: The value counts distinct people exposed to prospecting ads
Higher reach means a wider top-of-funnel audience
The sparkline shows the most recent 7-day reach trend
Compare reach growth against spend to gauge efficiency
Stalling reach despite spend suggests audience saturation
decisions_answered: How many new people did our prospecting reach?
Is our top-of-funnel audience expanding?
Are we saturating our prospecting pools?
Should we broaden targeting to grow reach?

meta-prospecting:cost_per1k_reach.title: Cost per 1k Reach
meta-prospecting:cost_per1k_reach.formula: Cost per 1k Reach = Meta prospecting spend ÷ reach × 1,000 (cost to reach 1,000 unique people)
meta-prospecting:cost_per1k_reach.formula_explanation: The cost to reach 1,000 unique people with prospecting ads. This is the core efficiency metric for top-of-funnel campaigns - lower is better. Target is under $6.00.
how_to_read: The value is the dollar cost to reach 1,000 distinct people
Lower means you are acquiring new-audience reach more cheaply
The sparkline traces the most recent 7-day efficiency trend
Under roughly $6 is a healthy prospecting benchmark
Rising cost per reach signals saturation or auction pressure
decisions_answered: How efficiently are we reaching new people?
Is our prospecting reach getting more or less expensive?
Are we hitting the under-$6 efficiency target?
Should we shift budget to cheaper-to-reach audiences?

meta-prospecting:avg_cpm.title: Avg CPM
meta-prospecting:avg_cpm.formula: Avg CPM = average of daily campaign CPM values across prospecting campaigns
meta-prospecting:avg_cpm.formula_explanation: The average cost per 1,000 impressions across prospecting campaigns, computed as the mean of daily CPM values. Industry benchmark is around $7.50. It reflects auction competition for impressions.
how_to_read: The value is the average dollar cost per 1,000 impressions
Lower CPM means cheaper impression buying in the auction
The sparkline shows the most recent 7-day CPM trend
Around $7.50 is a typical industry benchmark
CPM differs from cost per 1k reach - CPM counts impressions, not unique people
decisions_answered: How expensive are our prospecting impressions?
Is auction competition rising for our placements?
Are we above or below the industry CPM benchmark?
Is CPM pressure driving our overall costs up?

meta-prospecting:avg_frequency.title: Avg Frequency
meta-prospecting:avg_frequency.formula: Avg Frequency = average of daily campaign frequency values across prospecting campaigns
meta-prospecting:avg_frequency.formula_explanation: The average number of times each person saw the prospecting ads, taken as the mean of daily frequency. For prospecting, keep this under 2 - the goal is fresh reach, not repeated exposure.
how_to_read: The value is the average exposures per person
For prospecting, under 2 is ideal - you want new people, not repeats
The sparkline shows the most recent 7-day frequency trend
Rising frequency means you are re-showing ads instead of reaching new people
High prospecting frequency wastes budget and signals saturation
decisions_answered: Are our prospecting ads reaching new people or repeating?
Is frequency staying under the prospecting target of 2?
Are we saturating audiences and wasting top-of-funnel budget?
Should we expand audiences to lower frequency?

meta-retargeting:retargeting-spend.title: Retargeting Spend
meta-retargeting:retargeting-spend.formula: Retargeting Spend = total Meta spend across retargeting campaigns over the period
meta-retargeting:retargeting-spend.formula_explanation: Total spend across all Meta retargeting campaigns for the period. Retargeting spend is the investment in re-engaging people who already interacted with the brand or visited the site.
how_to_read: The large number is total dollars invested in retargeting
The sparkline traces daily retargeting spend over the recent period
The change percentage compares against the prior period
Retargeting typically carries higher ROAS than prospecting
Watch that spend does not outpace your re-engageable audience size
decisions_answered: How much are we investing in re-engaging past visitors?
Is retargeting spend growing or shrinking versus last period?
Is our bottom-of-funnel budget pacing as planned?
Are we over-spending against a limited retargeting pool?

meta-retargeting:retargeting-roas.title: Retargeting ROAS
meta-retargeting:retargeting-roas.formula: Retargeting ROAS = Meta retargeting conversion value ÷ retargeting spend
meta-retargeting:retargeting-roas.formula_explanation: Return on Ad Spend for retargeting campaigns - revenue generated per dollar spent. Retargeting reaches warm audiences, so the target is 5.0x or higher, well above prospecting.
how_to_read: The value is revenue returned per dollar of retargeting spend
Target is 5.0x or higher because retargeting hits warm audiences
The sparkline shows daily ROAS trends across the recent period
A declining trend with steady spend can signal audience fatigue
Compare retargeting ROAS to prospecting to confirm the funnel works
decisions_answered: Is our retargeting delivering the expected high returns?
Are we hitting the 5.0x+ retargeting ROAS target?
Is efficiency declining as we keep re-targeting the same people?
Should we scale retargeting budget given the returns?

meta-retargeting:retargeting-frequency.title: Retargeting Frequency
meta-retargeting:retargeting-frequency.formula: Retargeting Frequency = average of daily ad-set frequency across retargeting campaigns (exposures per person)
meta-retargeting:retargeting-frequency.formula_explanation: The average number of times each unique user saw retargeting ads. Optimal is 1-3 - enough to remind without annoying. Higher frequency risks fatigue and wasted spend on over-exposed users.
how_to_read: The value is the average exposures per retargeted person
An optimal range is 1 to 3 exposures
The sparkline shows daily frequency trends across the recent period
Rising frequency with falling CTR indicates fatigue
Very high frequency means you are over-serving a shrinking audience
decisions_answered: How often are retargeted users seeing our ads?
Is frequency in the optimal 1-3 range?
Are we over-exposing warm audiences and causing fatigue?
Should we add frequency caps or refresh creative?

meta-retargeting:retargeting-reach.title: Retargeting Reach
meta-retargeting:retargeting-reach.formula: Retargeting Reach = total unique users who saw retargeting ads over the period
meta-retargeting:retargeting-reach.formula_explanation: The total unique users who saw retargeting ads during the period. For retargeting this represents the size of the warm audience you are actively re-engaging.
how_to_read: The value counts distinct people reached by retargeting ads
The sparkline shows daily reach trends across the recent period
Reach here is bounded by your re-engageable audience pool
Flat reach with rising impressions means rising frequency
Shrinking reach can indicate the warm pool is being exhausted
decisions_answered: How many warm users are we re-engaging?
Is our retargeting audience pool growing or shrinking?
Are we exhausting the people available to retarget?
Do we need to feed more visitors into the retargeting funnel?

meta-retargeting:retargeting-ctr.title: Retargeting CTR
meta-retargeting:retargeting-ctr.formula: Retargeting CTR = average of daily ad-set click-through rate across retargeting campaigns (percentage)
meta-retargeting:retargeting-ctr.formula_explanation: Click-through rate for retargeting campaigns - the average percentage of impressions that resulted in clicks. Target is 2% or higher; warm audiences should engage more than cold ones.
how_to_read: The value is the average click-through rate as a percentage
Target is 2%+ since retargeting reaches engaged users
The sparkline shows daily CTR trends across the recent period
Falling CTR alongside rising frequency is a fatigue signal
Persistently low CTR may mean stale creative or a tired audience
decisions_answered: Are retargeted users still engaging with our ads?
Is CTR holding above the 2% retargeting target?
Is engagement declining as we re-show ads?
Do we need fresh creative to lift retargeting CTR?

meta-retargeting:retargeting-fatigue.title: Retargeting Fatigue
meta-retargeting:retargeting-fatigue.formula: Retargeting Fatigue Score = (historical-period CTR − current-period CTR) ÷ historical-period CTR × 100
meta-retargeting:retargeting-fatigue.formula_explanation: Measures CTR decline of the current period versus the comparison (historical) period. A higher percentage means engagement has dropped, indicating audience fatigue. The reference target is 15%.
how_to_read: The value is the percentage CTR decline versus the historical baseline
Higher percentages mean more fatigue - audiences are tuning out
Around 15% is used as the comparison reference threshold
The sparkline shows how the fatigue score moved across the recent period
A climbing fatigue score is the cue to refresh creative or cap frequency
decisions_answered: Are our retargeting audiences showing fatigue?
How much has engagement declined versus the baseline?
Which campaigns need creative refresh most urgently?
Should we cap frequency to slow the fatigue?

meta-creative:total_creatives.title: Total Creatives
meta-creative:total_creatives.formula: Total Creatives = count of distinct creative assets that ran in ads over the period
meta-creative:total_creatives.formula_explanation: The total number of unique creative assets used in ads during the selected period. This measures the breadth of your creative library actually in market.
how_to_read: The value counts distinct creatives that ran in the period
A larger library supports rotation and reduces fatigue risk
The sparkline traces the count of active creatives over recent days
Compare to active creatives to see how much of the library is spending
Too few creatives in market increases the risk of audience fatigue
decisions_answered: How many distinct creatives are we running?
Is our creative library broad enough to rotate?
Are we testing enough creative variety?
Do we have sufficient assets to prevent fatigue?

meta-creative:active_creatives.title: Active Creatives
meta-creative:active_creatives.formula: Active Creatives = count of distinct creatives with ad spend > 0 in the trailing 7 days
meta-creative:active_creatives.formula_explanation: The number of unique creatives that received ad spend in the last 7 days. This shows how much of your creative library is currently active versus dormant.
how_to_read: The value counts creatives that actually spent in the last 7 days
Compare against total creatives to gauge library utilization
The sparkline shows the daily active-creative count over recent days
A small active set against a large library means under-utilization
A shrinking active count can signal creatives being paused or exhausted
decisions_answered: How many creatives are actively running right now?
Are we utilizing our creative library or letting assets sit idle?
Is the number of active creatives enough to avoid fatigue?
Should we activate more assets from the library?

meta-creative:spend.title: Total Spend
meta-creative:spend.formula: Total Spend = total Meta advertising spend allocated to creatives over the period
meta-creative:spend.formula_explanation: Total advertising spend allocated to creatives during the selected period. This is the dollar investment behind the creative assets being analyzed.
how_to_read: The large number is total dollars spent across creatives
The sparkline traces daily creative spend over recent days
The change percentage compares against the prior period
Pair spend with CTR and CPC to judge creative efficiency
Concentrated spend on few creatives raises fatigue exposure
decisions_answered: How much are we investing across our creatives?
Is creative spend growing or shrinking versus last period?
Is spend concentrated or spread across the library?
Is the investment producing efficient engagement?

meta-creative:ctr.title: Avg CTR
meta-creative:ctr.formula: Avg CTR = Meta creative link clicks ÷ impressions × 100 (percentage)
meta-creative:ctr.formula_explanation: The average click-through rate across all creatives - link clicks as a percentage of impressions. It is the headline measure of how engaging your creative is.
how_to_read: The value is the percentage of impressions that produced link clicks
Higher CTR means more compelling creative
The sparkline shows the daily CTR trend over recent days
Falling CTR over time is a classic fatigue signal
Compare CTR across formats and copy lengths to find winners
decisions_answered: How engaging are our creatives overall?
Is creative engagement trending up or down?
Are our creatives fatiguing as CTR declines?
Which creative approaches drive the best click rates?

meta-creative:cpc.title: Avg CPC
meta-creative:cpc.formula: Avg CPC = Meta creative spend ÷ link clicks (cost per link click)
meta-creative:cpc.formula_explanation: The average cost per click across all creatives - total spend divided by total link clicks. Lower CPC means you are buying engagement more cheaply.
how_to_read: The value is the dollar cost of each link click
Lower CPC means more efficient click acquisition
The sparkline shows the daily CPC trend over recent days
Rising CPC with falling CTR points to creative fatigue
Compare CPC across formats and CTAs to find the cheapest engagement
decisions_answered: How much are we paying per click on our creatives?
Is click efficiency improving or worsening?
Are fatiguing creatives driving up CPC?
Which formats or CTAs deliver the cheapest clicks?

klaviyo-email:emails-sent.title: Emails Sent
klaviyo-email:emails-sent.formula: Emails Sent = total emails sent over the period
klaviyo-email:emails-sent.formula_explanation: Total number of marketing emails delivered to recipients over the selected period, summed from the email overview mart. This is the denominator behind every rate-based email KPI.
how_to_read: The main value shows total emails sent in the period
Compare against the prior period to gauge sending volume changes
The sparkline shows the daily send volume trend
Large swings usually reflect campaign sends layered on top of always-on flows
Click to break sends down by campaign vs. flow source
decisions_answered: How much email volume did we push this period?
Is our sending cadence growing or shrinking?
Did a big campaign drive an unusual spike in volume?

klaviyo-email:open-rate.title: Open Rate
klaviyo-email:open-rate.formula: Open Rate = unique opens ÷ emails sent × 100
klaviyo-email:open-rate.formula_explanation: Share of delivered emails that were opened at least once, computed as unique opens over total sends. It is the headline indicator of subject-line strength and list engagement.
how_to_read: The main value shows the period open rate as a percentage
Compare to the prior period to spot engagement shifts
The sparkline shows the daily open-rate trend
Persistent declines can signal deliverability or list-fatigue issues
Click to compare open rate across campaigns, flows, and content types
decisions_answered: Are subscribers opening our emails at a healthy rate?
Is engagement trending up or down this period?
Do we have a deliverability or list-quality problem to investigate?

klaviyo-email:click-rate.title: Click Rate
klaviyo-email:click-rate.formula: Click Rate = unique clicks ÷ emails sent × 100
klaviyo-email:click-rate.formula_explanation: Share of delivered emails that generated at least one click, computed as unique clicks over total sends. It measures how effectively email content drives action.
how_to_read: The main value shows the period click rate as a percentage
Compare to the prior period to track content effectiveness
The sparkline shows the daily click-rate trend
Low click rate with healthy opens points to weak content or offers
Click to compare performance by campaign, flow, and content type
decisions_answered: Is our email content driving clicks?
Are recipients acting on what we send?
Which content or offers should we double down on?

klaviyo-email:ctor.title: Click-to-Open Rate (CTOR)
klaviyo-email:ctor.formula: Click-to-Open Rate = unique clicks ÷ unique opens × 100
klaviyo-email:ctor.formula_explanation: Of the people who opened an email, the percentage that also clicked. CTOR isolates content quality from subject-line and deliverability effects, since it only counts engaged openers.
how_to_read: The main value shows the period click-to-open rate as a percentage
Compare to the prior period to judge content quality independent of opens
The sparkline shows the daily CTOR trend
A strong CTOR with a weak open rate means content is good but it is not getting opened
Click to compare CTOR across campaigns and content types
decisions_answered: Once opened, is our content compelling enough to drive clicks?
Is a click problem a content issue or an open issue?
Which creative resonates most with engaged readers?

klaviyo-email:bounce-rate.title: Bounce Rate
klaviyo-email:bounce-rate.formula: Bounce Rate = bounced emails ÷ emails sent × 100
klaviyo-email:bounce-rate.formula_explanation: Percentage of sent emails that could not be delivered and bounced back. A rising bounce rate erodes sender reputation and signals stale or low-quality list data.
how_to_read: The main value shows the period bounce rate as a percentage
Compare to the prior period to monitor list health
The sparkline shows the daily bounce-rate trend
Bounce rates above ~2% warrant list hygiene and re-permission work
Click to investigate deliverability health in more detail
decisions_answered: Is our list clean enough to protect sender reputation?
Are deliverability problems creeping in this period?
Do we need a list-hygiene or sunset campaign?

klaviyo-flows:active_flows.title: Active Flows
klaviyo-flows:active_flows.formula: Active Flows = count of distinct automation flows in live status that sent during the period
klaviyo-flows:active_flows.formula_explanation: Number of distinct automation flows currently live and sending. This counts only flows in a live status, not paused or draft automations.
how_to_read: The main value shows how many flows are live and sending
Compare to the prior period to track automation coverage
The sparkline shows how the active-flow count has trended
A shrinking count may mean key automations were paused
Click to see per-flow performance in the flow table
decisions_answered: How many automations are actively driving engagement?
Has anyone paused a flow that should be running?
Is our automation coverage growing over time?

klaviyo-flows:total_recipients.title: Total Recipients
klaviyo-flows:total_recipients.formula: Total Recipients = sum of sends across live flows over the period
klaviyo-flows:total_recipients.formula_explanation: Total number of recipients reached by live automation flows over the period, summed from the flow performance mart. It reflects the overall reach of always-on automation.
how_to_read: The main value shows total flow recipients in the period
Compare to the prior period to gauge automation reach
The sparkline shows the daily recipient trend
Spikes usually align with traffic or signup surges feeding the flows
Click to see which flows and trigger types drive the most reach
decisions_answered: How many people are our automations reaching?
Is flow reach growing alongside list growth?
Which trigger types drive the most recipients?

klaviyo-flows:avg_open_rate.title: Avg Open Rate
klaviyo-flows:avg_open_rate.formula: Avg Open Rate = average of each live flow-day's open rate (per-flow rates averaged, not a pooled opens ÷ sends)
klaviyo-flows:avg_open_rate.formula_explanation: Average open rate across live flows, computed as the mean of each flow-day's precomputed open rate (not a recomputed ratio of summed opens over sends). It summarizes automation engagement at a glance.
how_to_read: The main value shows the average flow open rate as a percentage
Compare to the prior period to track automation engagement
The sparkline shows the daily average open-rate trend
Because it averages per-flow rates, small flows count as much as large ones
Click to compare open rate by individual flow and trigger type
decisions_answered: How engaging are our automated flows overall?
Is flow engagement holding up over time?
Which flows are dragging the average down?

klaviyo-flows:avg_click_rate.title: Avg Click Rate
klaviyo-flows:avg_click_rate.formula: Avg Click Rate = average of each live flow-day's click rate (per-flow rates averaged, not a pooled clicks ÷ sends)
klaviyo-flows:avg_click_rate.formula_explanation: Average click rate across live flows, computed as the mean of each flow-day's precomputed click rate (not a recomputed ratio of summed clicks over sends). It measures how well flow content drives action.
how_to_read: The main value shows the average flow click rate as a percentage
Compare to the prior period to track content effectiveness
The sparkline shows the daily average click-rate trend
It averages per-flow rates, so each flow is weighted equally
Click to compare click rate by individual flow and trigger type
decisions_answered: Are our flows driving clicks effectively?
Is flow content performance steady over time?
Which flows need content refreshes?

klaviyo-sms:sms_sent.title: SMS Sent
klaviyo-sms:sms_sent.formula: SMS Sent = count of distinct received SMS messages over the period
klaviyo-sms:sms_sent.formula_explanation: Total number of SMS messages sent over the period, summed from the SMS performance mart. This is the volume denominator for the SMS rate metrics.
how_to_read: The main value shows total SMS sent in the period
Compare to the prior period to track SMS volume
The sparkline shows the daily send trend
Spikes typically reflect campaign blasts on top of always-on flows
Click to split volume by campaign vs. flow source

Is our SMS cadence growing or shrinking?
Did a campaign drive an unusual spike?

klaviyo-sms:click_rate.title: Click Rate
klaviyo-sms:click_rate.formula: SMS Click Rate = distinct clicked SMS ÷ distinct received SMS × 100
klaviyo-sms:click_rate.formula_explanation: Average SMS click rate over the period, computed as the mean of the precomputed daily click-rate values in the SMS mart. It measures how effectively SMS messages drive taps.
how_to_read: The main value shows the average SMS click rate as a percentage
Compare to the prior period to track engagement
The sparkline shows the daily click-rate trend
Because it averages daily rates, low-volume days carry equal weight
Click to see click rate by message and by best send time
decisions_answered: Are recipients clicking our SMS links?
Is SMS engagement steady this period?
Which messages or send times perform best?

klaviyo-sms:unsubscribe_rate.title: Unsubscribe Rate
klaviyo-sms:unsubscribe_rate.formula: SMS Unsubscribe Rate = distinct SMS opt-out events ÷ distinct received SMS × 100
klaviyo-sms:unsubscribe_rate.formula_explanation: Average SMS opt-out rate over the period, computed as the mean of the precomputed daily opt-out-rate values. A rising rate signals message fatigue or poor targeting.
how_to_read: The main value shows the average SMS opt-out rate as a percentage
Compare to the prior period to watch for list fatigue
The sparkline shows the daily opt-out trend
Sustained increases mean cadence or relevance needs attention
Click to investigate list health and send-time patterns

Do we need to cut cadence or improve targeting?

klaviyo-sms:revenue.title: SMS Revenue
klaviyo-sms:revenue.formula: SMS Revenue = total order value from Placed Order events attributed to recipients who received an SMS beforehand
klaviyo-sms:revenue.formula_explanation: Total revenue attributed to SMS over the period, summed from the SMS performance mart. It captures the direct financial return of the SMS channel.
how_to_read: The main value shows total SMS-attributed revenue for the period
Compare to the prior period to track channel contribution
The sparkline shows the daily revenue trend
Pair with SMS Sent to understand revenue efficiency
Click to see which messages drive the most revenue
decisions_answered: How much revenue is SMS generating?
Is the SMS channel growing in value?
Which messages drive the most revenue?

klaviyo-sms:revenue_per_sms.title: Revenue per SMS
klaviyo-sms:revenue_per_sms.formula: Revenue per SMS = SMS-attributed revenue ÷ total SMS sent
klaviyo-sms:revenue_per_sms.formula_explanation: Average revenue generated per SMS sent, dividing total attributed revenue by total sends. It is the core efficiency metric for justifying SMS send volume against carrier costs.
how_to_read: The main value shows revenue earned per SMS sent
Compare to the prior period to track efficiency
The sparkline shows the daily revenue-per-SMS trend
Falling efficiency with rising volume can mean over-sending

decisions_answered: Is each SMS send paying for itself?
Are we over-sending and diluting returns?

sms-performance:sms_sent.title: SMS Sent
sms-performance:sms_sent.formula: SMS Sent = count of distinct received SMS messages over the period
sms-performance:sms_sent.formula_explanation: Total number of SMS messages received by recipients over the period, counted from distinct 'Received SMS' events. This is the volume denominator for the SMS rate metrics.
how_to_read: The main value shows total SMS delivered in the period
Compare to the prior period to track SMS volume
The sparkline shows the send-volume trend
Spikes usually reflect campaign blasts on top of flows

sms-performance:click_rate.title: Click Rate
sms-performance:click_rate.formula: SMS Click Rate = distinct clicked SMS ÷ distinct received SMS × 100
sms-performance:click_rate.formula_explanation: Share of delivered SMS that were clicked, computed as distinct clicked messages over distinct received messages. It measures how effectively SMS drives taps.
how_to_read: The main value shows the SMS click rate as a percentage
Compare to the prior period to track engagement
The sparkline shows the click-rate trend
Low click rate suggests weak offers or message copy
Click to see click rate by message and send time
decisions_answered: Are recipients clicking our SMS links?

sms-performance:unsubscribe_rate.title: Unsubscribe Rate
sms-performance:unsubscribe_rate.formula: SMS Unsubscribe Rate = distinct SMS opt-out events ÷ distinct received SMS × 100
sms-performance:unsubscribe_rate.formula_explanation: Share of recipients who opted out of SMS marketing, computed as distinct unsubscribe events over distinct received messages. A rising rate signals message fatigue.
how_to_read: The main value shows the SMS opt-out rate as a percentage
Compare to the prior period to watch for list fatigue
The sparkline shows the opt-out trend
Sustained increases mean cadence or relevance needs attention

sms-performance:sms_revenue.title: SMS Revenue
sms-performance:sms_revenue.formula: SMS Revenue = total Placed Order value attributed to SMS engagement
sms-performance:sms_revenue.formula_explanation: Total revenue attributed to SMS over the period, summed from 'Placed Order' event values tied to SMS engagement. It captures the direct financial return of the SMS channel.
how_to_read: The main value shows total SMS-attributed revenue
Compare to the prior period to track channel contribution
The sparkline shows the revenue trend

decisions_answered: How much revenue is SMS generating?

sms-performance:revenue_per_sms.title: Revenue per SMS
sms-performance:revenue_per_sms.formula: Revenue per SMS = SMS-attributed revenue ÷ total SMS received
sms-performance:revenue_per_sms.formula_explanation: Average revenue generated per SMS delivered, dividing attributed revenue by distinct received messages. It is the core efficiency metric for the SMS channel.
how_to_read: The main value shows revenue earned per SMS sent
Compare to the prior period to track efficiency
The sparkline shows the revenue-per-SMS trend
Falling efficiency with rising volume can mean over-sending
Click to compare efficiency across messages
decisions_answered: Is each SMS send paying for itself?

magento-store-overview:revenue.title: Total Revenue
magento-store-overview:revenue.formula: Total Revenue = sum of gross order revenue over the period
magento-store-overview:revenue.formula_explanation: Total gross store revenue over the selected period, summed from the Magento daily mart. It is the top-line measure of store performance.
how_to_read: The main value shows total gross revenue for the period
Compare to the prior period for growth or decline

magento-store-overview:orders.title: Total Orders
magento-store-overview:orders.formula: Total Orders = count of all orders placed over the period
magento-store-overview:orders.formula_explanation: Total number of orders placed over the period, summed from the Magento daily mart. It is the core volume metric for the store.
how_to_read: The main value shows total orders for the period
Compare to the prior period to track demand
The sparkline shows the daily order trend

magento-store-overview:aov.title: Average Order Value
magento-store-overview:aov.formula: Average Order Value = gross revenue ÷ total orders
magento-store-overview:aov.formula_explanation: Average revenue per order over the period, dividing total gross revenue by total orders. It measures basket size and pricing power.
how_to_read: The main value shows average revenue per order
Compare to the prior period to track basket size
The sparkline shows the daily AOV trend

magento-store-overview:units_sold.title: Units Sold
magento-store-overview:units_sold.formula: Units Sold = total product units sold over the period
magento-store-overview:units_sold.formula_explanation: Total product units sold over the period, summed from the Magento daily mart. It measures physical demand independent of price.
how_to_read: The main value shows total units sold for the period
Compare to the prior period to track demand volume
The sparkline shows the daily units trend

magento-store-overview:unique_customers.title: Unique Customers
magento-store-overview:unique_customers.formula: Unique Customers = count of distinct customers who placed an order over the period
magento-store-overview:unique_customers.formula_explanation: Count of unique customers who ordered over the period, summed from the Magento daily mart. It reflects the breadth of the active buying base.
how_to_read: The main value shows unique purchasing customers for the period

magento-orders-fulfillment:total_orders.title: Total Orders
magento-orders-fulfillment:total_orders.formula: Total Orders = count of all orders placed over the period
magento-orders-fulfillment:total_orders.formula_explanation: Total orders placed over the period, the volume baseline for all fulfillment rates on this dashboard.
how_to_read: The main value shows total orders for the period

magento-orders-fulfillment:revenue.title: Total Revenue
magento-orders-fulfillment:revenue.formula: Total Revenue = sum of gross order revenue over the period
magento-orders-fulfillment:revenue.formula_explanation: Total gross order revenue over the period, summed from the Magento daily mart.
how_to_read: The main value shows total gross revenue for the period
Compare to the prior period for growth or decline
The sparkline shows the daily revenue trend
Pair with orders and AOV to interpret changes
Click to analyze revenue by payment and shipping method
decisions_answered: How much revenue did fulfilled orders generate?

magento-orders-fulfillment:aov.title: Average Order Value
magento-orders-fulfillment:aov.formula: Average Order Value = gross revenue ÷ total orders
magento-orders-fulfillment:aov.formula_explanation: Average revenue per order over the period, dividing total gross revenue by total orders.
how_to_read: The main value shows average revenue per order
Compare to the prior period to track basket size
The sparkline shows the daily AOV trend

magento-orders-fulfillment:completed_ord.title: Completed Orders
magento-orders-fulfillment:completed_ord.formula: Completed Orders = count of orders that reached the completed status over the period
magento-orders-fulfillment:completed_ord.formula_explanation: Number of orders that reached the completed state over the period. It is the success endpoint of the fulfillment funnel.
how_to_read: The main value shows orders that reached completion
Compare to total orders to gauge completion rate
The sparkline shows the daily completed-order trend
A growing gap from total orders signals fulfillment friction

magento-orders-fulfillment:processing_or.title: Processing Orders
magento-orders-fulfillment:processing_or.formula: Processing Orders = max(0, total orders − completed orders) — orders received but not yet completed (no separate canceled-order count is available, so canceled is treated as 0)
magento-orders-fulfillment:processing_or.formula_explanation: Orders that are neither completed nor canceled - i.e. still in-flight. At runtime the order-totals source lacks a canceled count, so the value effectively reduces to TOTAL_ORDERS minus COMPLETED_ORDERS (clamped at zero).
how_to_read: The main value shows orders currently in progress
A rising backlog can mean fulfillment is falling behind
The sparkline shows the in-progress trend
Compare to completed orders to judge throughput
Click to see where orders sit in the funnel
decisions_answered: How big is our in-flight order backlog?
Is fulfillment keeping pace with incoming orders?
Do we have a processing bottleneck?

formula_components.name: Intended
formula_components.formula: max(0, total orders − completed orders − canceled orders)

formula_components.name: Effective at runtime
formula_components.formula: max(0, total orders − completed orders) — no separate canceled-order count is available, so the canceled term is treated as 0

magento-orders-fulfillment:cancellation_.title: Cancellation Rate
magento-orders-fulfillment:cancellation_.formula: Cancellation Rate = canceled orders ÷ total orders × 100
magento-orders-fulfillment:cancellation_.formula_explanation: Share of orders that were canceled over the period. A rising rate points to stock-outs, payment failures, or customer-side cancellations.
how_to_read: The main value shows the cancellation rate as a percentage

magento-orders-fulfillment:refund_rate.title: Refund Rate
magento-orders-fulfillment:refund_rate.formula: Refund Rate = refunded orders ÷ total orders × 100
magento-orders-fulfillment:refund_rate.formula_explanation: Share of orders that were refunded over the period. A rising rate signals product, quality, or expectation problems.
how_to_read: The main value shows the refund rate as a percentage

magento-orders-fulfillment:cart_abandonm.title: Cart Abandonment Rate
magento-orders-fulfillment:cart_abandonm.formula: Cart Abandonment Rate = abandoned carts ÷ (abandoned carts + converted carts) × 100
magento-orders-fulfillment:cart_abandonm.formula_explanation: Share of carts that were started but never converted to an order, computed as abandoned carts over all carts (abandoned plus converted). It measures checkout friction.

magento-customer-analytics:total_custome.title: Total Customers
magento-customer-analytics:total_custome.formula: Total Customers = count of unique customers who ordered over the period
magento-customer-analytics:total_custome.formula_explanation: Total count of unique customers in the period, summed from the customer segments mart. It reflects the size of the buying base.

magento-customer-analytics:active_custom.title: Active Customers
magento-customer-analytics:active_custom.formula: Active Customers = count of unique customers who ordered over the period
magento-customer-analytics:active_custom.formula_explanation: Count of active customers in the period. Note: the backend currently computes this identically to Total Customers - there is no separate activity filter applied.

magento-customer-analytics:new_customers.title: New Customers
magento-customer-analytics:new_customers.formula: New Customers = count of first-time customers acquired over the period
magento-customer-analytics:new_customers.formula_explanation: Count of first-time customers acquired over the period, summed from the customer segments mart. It is the core acquisition metric.
how_to_read: The main value shows newly acquired customers for the period
Compare to the prior period to track acquisition
The sparkline shows the daily new-customer trend

magento-customer-analytics:repeat_rate.title: Repeat Rate
magento-customer-analytics:repeat_rate.formula: Repeat Rate = repeat customers ÷ unique customers × 100
magento-customer-analytics:repeat_rate.formula_explanation: Share of customers who placed more than one order, dividing repeat customers by unique customers. It measures loyalty and retention strength.
how_to_read: The main value shows the repeat-purchase rate as a percentage

magento-customer-analytics:average_ltv.title: Average LTV
magento-customer-analytics:average_ltv.formula: Average LTV = gross revenue ÷ unique customers
magento-customer-analytics:average_ltv.formula_explanation: Average lifetime value per customer in the period, dividing total gross revenue by unique customers. It measures the revenue worth of a typical customer.
how_to_read: The main value shows average revenue per customer

Click to see the LTV distribution by segment
decisions_answered: How much is a typical customer worth?
Is customer value growing over time?
How much can we afford to spend on acquisition?

magento-customer-analytics:avg_orders_pe.title: Avg Orders per Customer
magento-customer-analytics:avg_orders_pe.formula: Avg Orders per Customer = total orders ÷ unique customers
magento-customer-analytics:avg_orders_pe.formula_explanation: Average number of orders per customer in the period, dividing total orders by unique customers. It measures purchase frequency.
how_to_read: The main value shows average orders per customer
Compare to the prior period to track frequency
The sparkline shows the trend over time
Higher frequency lifts LTV without new acquisition
Click to explore RFM frequency segments
decisions_answered: How often does a typical customer order?
Is purchase frequency improving?
Can we drive more repeat orders from existing customers?

magento-customer-analytics:avg_days_betw.title: Avg Days Between Orders
magento-customer-analytics:avg_days_betw.formula: Avg Days Between Orders = average of the daily per-segment average gap between a customer's orders, over rows that have a positive value
magento-customer-analytics:avg_days_betw.formula_explanation: Average gap in days between a customer's orders, computed as the mean of daily AVG_DAYS_BETWEEN_ORDERS values over rows with data. Shorter gaps indicate more frequent purchasing.
how_to_read: The main value shows the average days between repeat orders
Compare to the prior period to track purchase cadence
The sparkline shows the trend over time
A shrinking gap means customers are buying more often
Click to explore frequency and recency segments
decisions_answered: How long until a customer typically buys again?
Is repurchase cadence speeding up or slowing down?
When should re-engagement campaigns fire?

magento-customer-analytics:guest_checkou.title: Guest Checkout Rate
magento-customer-analytics:guest_checkou.formula: Guest Checkout Rate = orders placed by guest customers ÷ total orders × 100
magento-customer-analytics:guest_checkou.formula_explanation: Share of orders placed without a registered account, computed from MART_MAGENTO_DAILY. A high rate represents an opportunity to convert guests into known, retainable customers.
how_to_read: The main value shows the guest-checkout share of orders
Compare to the prior period to track account adoption
The sparkline shows the guest-rate trend
Lowering the rate grows the addressable retention base
Click to explore the customer mix in more detail
decisions_answered: What share of orders come from guests?
Are we converting guests into registered customers?
How much retention upside is locked in guest checkout?

website-performance:sessions.title: Sessions
website-performance:sessions.formula: Sessions = sum of website sessions over the period
website-performance:sessions.formula_explanation: Total website sessions over the period, summed from the website performance mart (GA4 sourced). It is the top-of-funnel traffic volume metric.
how_to_read: The main value shows total sessions for the period
Compare to the prior period to track traffic
The sparkline shows the daily session trend
Pair with users to gauge sessions per visitor
Click to break traffic down by channel and source
decisions_answered: How much traffic did the site get?
Is traffic growing or declining?
Which channels are driving sessions?

website-performance:users.title: Users
website-performance:users.formula: Users = sum of unique users over the period
website-performance:users.formula_explanation: Total unique users who visited over the period, summed from the website performance mart (GA4 sourced). It measures audience reach.
how_to_read: The main value shows total users for the period
Compare to the prior period to track reach
The sparkline shows the daily user trend
Compare to sessions to estimate repeat visits
Click to explore audiences and demographics
decisions_answered: How many distinct visitors reached the site?
Is our audience growing?
Are visitors returning or mostly new?

website-performance:bounce_rate.title: Bounce Rate
website-performance:bounce_rate.formula: Bounce Rate = (1 − engaged sessions ÷ sessions) × 100 (weighted across the period)
website-performance:bounce_rate.formula_explanation: Share of sessions that were not engaged, computed as one minus the engaged-session ratio. It is a weighted average across the period, not a simple mean of daily rates.
how_to_read: The main value shows the bounce rate as a percentage
Compare to the prior period to track landing quality
The sparkline shows the daily bounce-rate trend
High bounce points to landing-page or traffic-quality issues
Click to analyze bounce by channel and landing page
decisions_answered: Are visitors engaging or leaving immediately?
Is landing-page quality holding up?
Which channels send low-quality traffic?

website-performance:engagement_rate.title: Engagement Rate
website-performance:engagement_rate.formula: Engagement Rate = (engaged sessions ÷ sessions) × 100 (weighted across the period)
website-performance:engagement_rate.formula_explanation: Share of sessions that were engaged, computed as engaged sessions over total sessions. It is a weighted average across the period and is the inverse of bounce rate.
how_to_read: The main value shows the engagement rate as a percentage
Compare to the prior period to track content stickiness
The sparkline shows the daily engagement trend
Rising engagement usually precedes better conversion
Click to compare engagement by channel and device
decisions_answered: Are visitors engaging with the site?
Is engagement improving over time?
Which channels deliver the most engaged traffic?

website-performance:revenue.title: Revenue
website-performance:revenue.formula: Revenue = sum of net revenue from Magento orders over the period (revenue source of truth)
website-performance:revenue.formula_explanation: Total net revenue over the period. Note: revenue on this dashboard is sourced from the Magento daily mart (the revenue source of truth), not from GA4 attribution.
how_to_read: The main value shows total net revenue for the period
Compare to the prior period to track sales
The sparkline shows the daily revenue trend
Pair with sessions to understand revenue per visit
Click to connect traffic to revenue by channel
decisions_answered: How much revenue did the site generate?
Is revenue tracking with traffic trends?
Which channels convert traffic into revenue?

cro:conversion_rate.title: Conversion Rate
cro:conversion_rate.formula_explanation: Share of sessions that ended in a purchase, computed as total purchases over total sessions from the funnel mart. It is the headline measure of site conversion effectiveness.
how_to_read: The main value shows the conversion rate as a percentage
Compare to the prior period to track effectiveness
The sparkline shows the daily conversion trend
